steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The passage ends with "Consequently, ______" — a signal that the blank must be the logical conclusion drawn from the preceding evidence. That evidence: last-mile delivery faces multiple persistent challenges (complex routes, slow robots, navigation obstacles), and current innovations have not overcome them. The blank must state the most reasonable inference about near-term prospects.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the use of autonomous delivery robots may enable delivery companies to meet consumers' expectations now but likely is not viable as a permanent solution.": The passage says robots are already constrained and struggling — they cannot currently meet expectations. This contradicts the evidence. ✗
  • "innovations in last-mile logistics seem poised to increase consumers' expectations for rapid delivery.": The passage says innovations have been constrained, not that they will raise expectations. This reverses the causal direction. ✗
  • "delivery companies should invest more funds in proven long-distance transport technologies than in untested last-mile solutions.": The passage makes no comparison of investment priorities. This is a recommendation not supported by anything stated. ✗
  • "in the near term, delivery companies are unlikely to overcome the impediments associated with last-mile logistics.": "Constrained" innovations and persistent obstacles lead directly to this conclusion. It follows from all the evidence without overstating or adding unsupported claims. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
